rag_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes and component type contracts in type scopes.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for output dependency contracts.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to prevent unresolved references.
Ensure AGREE scalar types match AADL conventions (int, real, bool) to avoid resolution errors.
Do not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE contracts are present in the current AADL scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes.
Do not reference package-qualified identifiers not present in the model's dependencies.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
Component type vs. implementation scope distinctions in model fusion
